context_blocks.title: Runtime context 1
context_blocks.text: ok so i have all the cli worker

runtime_understanding.runtime_summary: This repository is a Python-based documentation-generation service (AutoDocX) composed of a FastAPI backend (app.*) and a Streamlit UI (ui/streamlit_app.py). The backend exposes many POST/GET endpoints to scan local projects, save scans, compare scans, generate LLM-based project understandings, and produce standalone HTML documentation. The Streamlit UI calls those FastAPI endpoints (requests.post / requests.get) and also provides a special endpoint that accepts runtime/context blocks and screenshots. The project integrates with third-party LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic/Claude) via a provider factory and requires provider selection and API keys via environment variables. Local file storage (workspace paths) is used for saved scans, understandings, documents, and uploaded runtime assets. The provided screenshots show the developer running both the Streamlit UI (port 8501) and the FastAPI app with uvicorn (port 8000), and also show uvicorn hot-reload activity when editing app/api/project_routes.py.

tooling_stack.purpose: Primary implementation language for backend services, project scanner, analyzers, and Streamlit UI.
tooling_stack.evidence: Repository modules are Python (app/*.py, ui/streamlit_app.py). Project statistics show 27 python modules, 45 classes, and many functions. Many modules are named app.services.* and ui.streamlit_app.

tooling_stack.purpose: HTTP API exposing endpoints for scanning, comparing, building documentation, and runtime-context ingestion.
tooling_stack.evidence: app/api/project_routes.py defines many API routes (e.g. POST /api/projects/documentation/sync, POST /api/projects/documentation/sync-with-context, GET /health). app.main registers routes; module references list many router.post and router.get handlers.

tooling_stack.purpose: ASGI server used to run the FastAPI app (development with --reload).
tooling_stack.evidence: Screenshots show the command 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' and uvicorn logs: 'Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000' and startup/reload logs. app.main is the referenced app module.

tooling_stack.purpose: Developer-facing UI to call the API endpoints and upload runtime context blocks/screenshots.
tooling_stack.evidence: ui/streamlit_app.py exists and contains functions to build multipart payloads and call endpoints. Screenshot shows 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' and 'Uvicorn server started on 0.0.0.0:8501' (Streamlit default) with Local URL http://localhost:8501.

tooling_stack.purpose: Persist scans, understandings, HTML documents and runtime assets (screenshots) under workspace paths.
tooling_stack.evidence: Modules: app.services.scan_storage docstring shows scans saved under workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json. understanding_storage and document_storage docstrings reference workspace/understandings and workspace/documents paths. app.core.config contains path constants and create_required_directories.

tooling_stack.purpose: Generate narrative project understandings and optionally analyze runtime screenshots (vision-capable LLM).
tooling_stack.evidence: app.services.llm.factory, app.services.llm.openai_provider, app.services.llm.anthropic_provider modules exist. app.core.config exposes get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model. project_understanding_service and runtime_context_analyzer call provider APIs.

tooling_stack.purpose: Streamlit UI makes requests.post/requests.get calls to the FastAPI backend for sync, sync-with-context and history endpoints.
tooling_stack.evidence: ui/streamlit_app.sync_documentation and sync_documentation_with_context use requests.post; functions parse JSON responses and build multipart files (build_context_blocks_payload).

runtime_flow.title: Start the backend API
runtime_flow.description: Start the FastAPI application with Uvicorn. Development uses hot reload (uvicorn app.main:app --reload). The server listens on 127.0.0.1:8000 by default in the screenshot.
runtime_flow.evidence: Screenshot 2 shows 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' and 'Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000'. app.main module contains root and /health routes.

runtime_flow.title: Start the Streamlit UI
runtime_flow.description: Run the Streamlit front-end which provides an interface to call the backend API endpoints and upload runtime context. The Streamlit app runs on port 8501 by default.
runtime_flow.evidence: Screenshot 1 shows the command 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' and Streamlit serving on Local URL http://localhost:8501. ui/streamlit_app.py includes functions to build and send context_blocks and screenshots.

runtime_flow.title: User triggers project scan or sync
runtime_flow.description: From the UI (or direct API), request endpoints are called to scan a local project path or synchronize documentation. There are two main endpoints: a single-request sync (/api/projects/documentation/sync) and an extended sync that accepts runtime context and image files (/api/projects/documentation/sync-with-context).
runtime_flow.evidence: app/api/project_routes.py exposes POST /documentation/sync and POST /documentation/sync-with-context with signatures and docstrings describing scanning, saving scans, creating understandings, comparing scans, and generating documents. ui.streamlit_app.sync_documentation and sync_documentation_with_context show the UI calling these endpoints.

runtime_flow.title: Project scanner and analyzer produce structured scan
runtime_flow.description: ProjectScanner parses the filesystem, reads Python files with PythonCodeParser, computes hashes, counts files, and builds module relationships. The analyzer collects routes, functions, classes and other developer-oriented metadata used downstream.
runtime_flow.evidence: Modules: app.services.project_scanner, app.services.code_parser, app.services.project_analyzer. project_routes calls project_scanner.scan_project and then scan_storage.save_scan.

runtime_flow.title: LLM-based understanding generation (optional / configurable)
runtime_flow.description: When generating an understanding, the app selects the configured LLM provider (get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model) and calls project_understanding_service.generate_understanding which uses the provider to get narrative summaries. Generated understanding JSON is saved to understanding_storage.
runtime_flow.evidence: Routes: POST /{project_name}/understand uses get_llm_provider, get_llm_api_key, get_llm_model and project_understanding_service.generate_understanding. LLM providers exist under app.services.llm.*

runtime_flow.title: Document generation
runtime_flow.description: DocumentBuilder.build_html uses the stored understanding to construct a standalone HTML documentation bundle. DocumentStorage.save_document persists metadata and HTML, and DocumentStorage.prepare_runtime_assets stores any uploaded screenshots, returning an asset directory / batch id.
runtime_flow.evidence: project_routes.generate_project_document calls understanding_storage.get_understanding and document_builder.build_html then document_storage.save_document. For runtime assets, project_routes.sync_project_documentation_with_context calls document_storage.prepare_runtime_assets and passes runtime_context into document generation.

runtime_flow.title: Runtime/context ingestion (images and notes)
runtime_flow.description: The UI can attach ordered runtime context blocks and image files. The backend accepts multipart form input, saves uploaded screenshots, maps screenshot_index to blocks, optionally asks a vision-capable LLM to analyze screenshot+notes (runtime_context_analyzer), and merges new runtime context with previously attached context when creating new document versions.
runtime_flow.evidence: sync_project_documentation_with_context signature accepts Form fields additional_context and context_blocks_json and File screenshots. Helper functions in project_routes: _save_uploaded_screenshots_to_temp, document_storage.prepare_runtime_assets, _build_runtime_context_from_blocks, _analyze_runtime_context_with_llm, _merge_runtime_contexts.

runtime_flow.title: Serve and open generated HTML docs
runtime_flow.description: Generated HTML documentation files are stored on disk and can be opened or downloaded via GET /{project_name}/documents/{document_id}/html which returns a FileResponse.
runtime_flow.evidence: project_routes.get_project_document_html returns FileResponse using document_storage.get_document_file. ui.streamlit_app.build_document_url composes the URL to open a generated HTML document.

screenshot_insights.title: Streamlit UI started (port 8501)
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: Terminal output after running 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py'. Logs indicate the Streamlit (Uvicorn) server is serving the UI on 0.0.0.0:8501 with Local URL http://localhost:8501 and a network URL.
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Confirms the repository contains a Streamlit front-end (ui/streamlit_app.py) used to interact with the backend. The Streamlit UI is the likely place a developer will upload runtime context blocks and screenshots for 'sync-with-context'.

screenshot_insights.title: FastAPI backend started with uvicorn on port 8000 and active API traffic
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: Terminal output showing the command 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload', uvicorn serving on http://127.0.0.1:8000, reloader and server process IDs, and many GET requests to endpoints like /api/projects/AutoDocX/documents returning 200 OK.
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Confirms how to start the backend (uvicorn app.main:app --reload) and that the API endpoints are reachable at port 8000. The logs show production-style request handling and that client code (likely the Streamlit UI) is successfully calling the API.

screenshot_insights.title: Hot-reload activity when editing project_routes.py
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: Repeated uvicorn reloader messages: 'StatReload detected changes in app\api\project_routes.py. Reloading...' with server shutdown and restart cycles and new server process IDs.
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Shows the development workflow uses uvicorn --reload, so code edits to API modules cause automatic restarts. This is useful for iterative development but also explains transient downtime between restarts.

operational_notes: Backend default address in screenshots: http://127.0.0.1:8000 (uvicorn). Streamlit UI default address: http://localhost:8501. Expect two services to run locally simultaneously.

Two startup methods: 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' to run the FastAPI backend; 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' to run the UI. The UI calls the backend using configurable API base URL (ui/streamlit_app.normalize_api_url).

To generate LLM-based understandings or run vision-based runtime analysis you must set environment variables for the chosen provider. app.core.config.get_llm_provider reads LLM_PROVIDER and provider-specific API keys are required (OpenAI/Anthropic).

Saved artifacts are stored on the local workspace paths (workspace/scans, workspace/understandings, workspace/documents). DocumentStorage and ScanStorage docstrings indicate layout—ensure proper filesystem permissions.

sync-with-context endpoint accepts multipart form data: 'project_path' (form), 'additional_context' (form), 'context_blocks_json' (form), and multiple 'screenshots' file parts. UI builds files and context_blocks_json with screenshot_index mapping (ui.streamlit_app.build_context_blocks_payload).

The runtime/context pipeline de-duplicates screenshots using a signature based on asset_file/html_src/filename (see _screenshot_signature), and merges previous runtime contexts into new documents (_merge_runtime_contexts).

risks_or_gaps.title: Missing LLM provider configuration or API keys
risks_or_gaps.description: The code requires an LLM provider (openai/anthropic/claude) and provider-specific API keys set via environment variables. If these are not present or invalid, endpoints that call the LLM (understand_project, update_project_document, _analyze_runtime_context_with_llm) will raise ValueError or return HTTP errors.

risks_or_gaps.title: No evidence of cloud storage or concurrency controls
risks_or_gaps.description: DocumentStorage, ScanStorage, and UnderstandingStorage appear to use local filesystem paths under 'workspace'. If multiple users/processes use the same workspace concurrently (or when deployed), there is no visible coordination or external storage backing in the provided context.

risks_or_gaps.title: Vision-capable LLM requirement for runtime context analysis
risks_or_gaps.description: Runtime screenshot analysis (_analyze_runtime_context_with_llm) calls a vision-capable LLM. Without a model/provider that supports images, the analysis step will be skipped or fail. The code expects this behavior but require documentation and env setup for vision models.

risks_or_gaps.title: Duplicate screenshot filenames in provided context payload
risks_or_gaps.description: User-supplied screenshot metadata shows two images with the same original_filename 'context_block_001.png' mapped to screenshot_001.png twice. This can cause deduplication to drop one image or mis-map screenshot_index. The project has signature-based dedupe but behavior should be tested.

risks_or_gaps.title: Unclear startup sequence for required directories
risks_or_gaps.description: app.core.config.create_required_directories exists to create workspace directories, but it's not visible from provided context whether main.py calls it on startup. If directories are missing, save operations may fail.

risks_or_gaps.title: Hot-reload transient downtime during development
risks_or_gaps.description: Uvicorn --reload restarts server processes on code changes (seen for app/api/project_routes.py). During reloads in dev environment endpoints are briefly unavailable—tests or long-running clients should account for that.
